symptoms.symptom_name: Polyuria with nocturia over three weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osmotic diuresis from sustained hyperglycaemia is a cardinal feature of new-onset diabetes.
Compensatory thirst driven by osmotic fluid loss and hyperglycaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unexplained rapid weight loss despite polyphagia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Catabolism of fat and muscle from absolute insulin deficiency causes weight loss even with increased eating — near-specific for type 1 onset in a child.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe fatigue and lethargy (unable to attend school)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific but consistent with progressive metabolic derangement and dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent vomiting, unable to keep fluids down

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ketosis and gastric stasis cause vomiting; also the shared feature that anchors clinicians toward gastroenteritis.
Vomiting alone is non-specific; must not be read in isolation.
Diffuse non-localised pain without focal peritonism argues against a surgical cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep, rapid, sighing (Kussmaul) respirations at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Respiratory compensation for severe metabolic acidosis; a hallmark red flag of established ketoacidosis.
Exhaled acetone from ketosis; near-specific for ketoacidosis and carer-detectable, so admissible over telehealth as a reported observation.
Altered conscious level marks severe DKA and cerebral-oedema risk; also an airway (aspiration) risk with vomiting.
Osmotic lens changes from hyperglycaemia cause transient blurred vision.

symptoms.symptom_name: Carer-observable dehydration — sunken eyes, drowsiness, no oral intake tolerated

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe dehydration from osmotic diuresis and vomiting; carer can report sunken eyes and drowsiness.
Afebrile status makes primary sepsis less likely, though DKA can be precipitated by infection and the two can co-exist.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia, hypotension and delayed capillary refill (hypovolaemic shock)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective distributive/hypovolaemic shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from drowsiness and dehydration, not from a measured blood pressure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Capillary blood glucose above meter limit (reads HIGH, >33.3 mmol/L)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Extreme hyperglycaemia remains unconfirmed; but in an undiagnosed patient with no home meter this is obtained by ambulance/hospital, not by the AI Doctor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extreme hyperglycaemia with the clinical picture confirms hyperglycaemic crisis — a clinician/hospital measurement, sealed.

investigation_details.expected_result: Critically elevated (~6.2 mmol/L; >3 indicates significant ketoacidosis)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Ketoacidosis remains biochemically unconfirmed; a hospital/ambulance measurement, sealed from telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked ketonaemia with hyperglycaemia and acidosis defines DKA — sealed clinician measurement.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Type classification is delayed; does not change the acute emergency management and is done after stabilisation by endocrinology.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the autoimmune type-1 aetiology retrospectively; not available in real time and irrelevant to the acute escalation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss and fatigue over weeks are the textbook presentation of new-onset type 1 diabetes and date the underlying disease.
Vomiting, diffuse abdominal pain, Kussmaul respirations, fruity breath and drowsiness together indicate established, severe ketoacidosis — an immediate emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate ambulance transfer for bedside glucose, blood ketones, venous blood gas and electrolytes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades DKA (glucose, ketones, pH/bicarbonate, potassium) and guides fluid/insulin protocol — obtained by paramedics/hospital, not by the AI Doctor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes including potassium and renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Potassium derangement is the main cause of fatal arrhythmia during DKA and must be measured before and during insulin therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Islet autoantibodies (anti-GAD, IA-2, ZnT8) and HbA1c
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm the autoimmune type-1 aetiology after stabilisation; not part of the acute emergency.
Contraindicated — a vomiting, drowsy child cannot safely take oral fluids (aspiration) and oral fluids cannot correct the deficit; escalate for IV therapy instead.
